title: Unexpected null bytes returned in podman rest api logs endpoint
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug


**Description**

I'm trying to get the response of the log endpoint i.e `/libpod/{name}/logs` with the stdout set to true using python's request library, but the response returns a byte object which has unexpected null bytes in it. But on running podman logs from the terminal the ouput is as expected

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.Start a Python container with a command such as `python helloworld.py`

2.Send a request to the podman rest api **logs** endpoint using the container id and stdout set to true


**Describe the results you received:**
This is the raw output of the request:
```
b'\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01*\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x02**\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x03***\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x04****\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x05*****\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x06******\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x07*******\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x08********\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\t*********'
```
This is the decoded output to utf-8:
`***********************************     *********`

**Describe the results you expected:**
The output to be expected is:
```
*
**
***
****
*****
******
*******
********
*********
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Thu Jan  1 05:30:00 1970
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body: Can you pipe the output from podman logs into `od -t c` to check if it also contains the 0 bytes.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Since we got no more feedback, closing on insufficient data.  Reopen if the issue still exists and you have the data.

I've got a similar behavior.
starting a alpine container with following CMD `["sh", "-c", 'echo "asdf" ; sleep 3']` several times, should response something like this:

```
asdf
asdf
asdf
...
```

#### Response from curl
```
curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock \
--url 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/containers/test-alpine/logs?stdout=true' \
--request GET \
--output -

```

response:
```
asdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdfasdf
```
#### Response from podman logs

```
podman logs test-alpine | od -t c
```

response
```
0000000   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a
0000020   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s
0000040   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d
0000060   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f
0000100  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n
0000120   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n   a
0000140   s   d   f  \n   a   s   d   f  \n
```

So the the response of podman logs looks fine for me
Is the problem here the lack of newline characters in the return from the API?
It looks like logs is using the same binary stream format as Attach, so I think this looks expected - the message format is binary and I bet a lot of it is being treated as nonprintables.

Okay, then I understood the  [documentation](https://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html#operation/ContainerLogsLibpod ) in the wrong way. I expected a stream in the format as it get back like the create container response. But with stream a byte stream is meant.

Sorry for requesting again, but in my opinion the endpoint does not behave as expected.

If I have a look to a container vie command line everything looks as it should:
`podman logs affectionate_black | od -t c`

Response:
```
0000000  \n   -       -       -       -       -       -       -       -
0000020       -       -       -       -       -       -       -       -
*
0000120  \n   N   o       s   i   m   u   l   a   t   e   d       r   e
0000140   n   e   w   a   l   s       w   e   r   e       a   t   t   e
0000160   m   p   t   e   d   .  \n   -       -       -       -       -
0000200       -       -       -       -       -       -       -       -
*
0000300       -       -       -  \n
0000307
```

if I request via curl it response not as expected:
`curl --unix-socket /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock --url 'http://d/v3.0.0/libpod/containers/affectionate_black/logs?stdout=true' --request GET --output -`

Response:
```O- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -%No simulated renewals were attempted.O- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -```

If I request via python script (requests lib) and pprint the response I get this:
```
('\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00O- - - - - - - - '
 '- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - '
 '-\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00%No simulated renewals were '
 'attempted.\x01\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00O- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - '
 '- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -\x02\x00\x00\x00\x00\x00\x008Saving '
 'debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log')
```

the print result looks same as the curl request

And finally, `podman logs affectionate_black` response this:

```
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
No simulated renewals were attempted.
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - -
Saving debug log to /var/log/letsencrypt/letsencrypt.log
```

Very strange is the fact that the last row of the log is cut of in the python print request, the curl request and the podman logs affectionate_black | od -t c
Are you talking about the newline? Newlines are not directly included in the output returned by the logs endpoint, they're instead added automatically between full log lines as sent by the endpoint.
